subject: Re: hybrid data processing
body:
Hi Jesse,
If ignored it should take it from the mdocs, do you see a dose listed to the right of each tilt-series?
The intensity shouldn’t throw warp off, it’s pretty good about normalising when it needs to :-)
I don’t know of any existing tool, I would likely write a Python script if doing this myself!
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Nov 21, 2023, at 07:33, mrJ <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿I guess one thing I'm not sure of is how to handle the tilt series import/export panel where it asks "default dose per tilt". 
Also another issue with this approach is the mean and SD of the untilted HD image, is there any tool (probably in IMOD) that you can think of to reduce the intensities of the HD image to match the rest of the tilt series?  I''ll try without this step out of curiosity, but I'm guess it will throw off WARP if one tilt has way stronger signal/contrast than all the others.
thanks again
Jesse
On Friday, November 17, 2023 at 6:33:57 PM UTC+1 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
Hey Jesse,
It should be enough to have two separate instances of Warp for preprocessing, one for your high dose images and one for your low dose images. These can then be combined by copying the files from one into the other. Everything downstream should then work as expected, the only thing you will need to take care of is making sure the cumulative dose is dealt with correctly, if it's read in from your mdocs you should be good to go :-)
Cheers,
Alister
On Thu, 16 Nov 2023 at 22:55, mrJ <
bluu...@gmail.com
> wrote:
dear WARP commnunity,
I'd like to try processing hybrid cryo-EM data: a single high-dose (HD) image and a typical tilt series.  The HD image has more frames, higher dose, and will be aligned with patches -- quite different than how the tilts are corrected. Are there any clever ideas on how I might implement these two data types in the WARP pipeline?  One option I can see is to align the HD image with Motioncorr outside of WARP, then maybe overwrite the WARP aligned micrograph in the "average" folder.  However I'm not sure how WARP will then deal with tomo/subtomo reconstruction -- for on-the-fly reconstructions does it use the frames or the averaged micrograph?  If the latter then I guess it just might work.   However.... if I try running M later on then I will certainly somehow need the frames too.  Perhaps for that I could simply generate a "fake" tilt movie by summing my frames (e.g. 40) into groups which would match the frames for a tilt (e.g. 8).  So I'd separate out 5 frames at a time, correct it in MotionCorr, do that 8 times, then create a stack with 8 micrographs.  I'd have to also correct the pixel intensity but that's a different problem, for now just trying to incorporate it into WARP.   
thanks!
Jesse
